blocks.content: Neide and Iruki teleported in front of the Uncrossable Bridge's cliff.
Their eyes burned with fury after witnessing Shina's humiliation.

blocks.speaker: Neide
blocks.content: How dare you lay a hand on our teacher…!

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Oh? There were some sane ones left? Typical mages…
Lucas was equally annoyed.
A so-called grand mage had made the mistake of missing his targets.
Moreover, the ones still sane were now his responsibility to deal with.

blocks.speaker: Lucas
blocks.content: Was that woman your teacher? My bad. She's probably almost reached the bottom by now. The ground, I mean. Heh.
The moment Lucas finished speaking, Shirone, holding Shina in his arms, teleported up from below the cliff.
His gaze was no different from his friends'. How depraved did one have to be to throw someone off a cliff?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I won't forgive you.
Lucas didn't even bother listening.
The situation escalating was just annoying.

blocks.speaker: Lucas
blocks.content: Hey. What's the plan? Is this part of the operation?
Lucas made the responsibility clear.
It might come up during the final payment.
Canis didn't evade it either.
As the leader in Arcane's absence, it was his duty.

blocks.speaker: Lucas
blocks.content: Arin, stay back.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: But Canis…

blocks.speaker: Lucas
blocks.content: You don't need to intervene. Focus on controlling the people. I'll handle this.
Maintaining mental control over hundreds while fighting was impossible.
As Arin hid among the crowd, Canis approached Shirone's group, unburdened.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Who are you? How did you break free from the mental control?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Dark magic is outdated. There are plenty of counters.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Nonsense. If it were that easy, the ones behind us wouldn't be affected.
True…
Canis looked up at the sun.
At the peak, the light was still strong.
But as a grand mage's disciple, there was no need to negotiate with students.

blocks.speaker: Lucas
blocks.content: Hey, what's the plan? This wasn't in the contract. I'll handle it for double the pay.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I don't need help. I'll do it alone.

blocks.speaker: Lucas
blocks.content: Suit yourself.
Lucas clicked his tongue and stepped back, though his true intent was the opposite.
He'd pretend to stay out of it, then strike from behind once the fight started.

blocks.speaker: Lucas
blocks.content: Come at me. I'm busy.
Shirone and Canis closed the distance.
Shirone sensed Canis's Spirit Zone—a 50-meter sphere, similar to his own.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I'll handle this. You stop that swordsman.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: That guy? He doesn't seem like he'll interfere.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: You never know. He's vicious enough to throw Shina off a cliff.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Don't believe a word he says.
